diary: Woke up early with the sun streaming through my bedroom window—perfect timing for a Monday. Made a strong espresso and watched the light hit my toast before heading to the Ballard office. The morning flew by sketching out sight lines for the new Greenwood Park expansion. Around eleven, I ditched the desk and headed downtown to catch the USA-Wales match at this cozy bar near Pike Place. The energy was electric, even if the game wasn't always pretty. Grabbed a decent fish and chips after, then drove back to finish up client presentations. The afternoon felt productive but also a little blurry—those long stretches at the computer, you know? On my way home I swung by the Ballard Goodwill and found this vintage landscape photography book for three bucks. Small win. Felt good to end the day with something tactile in my hands.
thought_process: The World Cup match pulled me away from work earlier than I'd normally take a break, but honestly, I needed the reset. I've been buried in renderings for weeks, and something about watching live soccer with other people in the room charged me back up. The light rail closure didn't affect me since I drive, but I noticed the downtown bars were packed—that ripple effect of the match time. My mood shifted from focused-but-tired this morning to genuinely energized after lunch, then settled into steady-productive by evening. The Goodwill stop was impulsive, but it felt right; I've been all screens and meetings, and I wanted to remember why I got into landscape design in the first place. Those old photographs reminded me.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
